nation: Madawaska Maliseet First Nation New Brunswick
one_time_description: Extraordinary item recognized in fiscal year 2022 - likely a treaty or land settlement payment
economic_notes: Band-owned businesses include St. John Valley Gas Bar, Smoke Signal (tobacco/convenience), and Madawaska Maliseet Economic Development Corporation. Investment in BeeHigh Vital Elements Inc. (licensed medical marijuana producer, 15.78% ownership, 2,857,143 common shares) at cost of $1,000,000 with indicators of impairment noted by auditors.
notes: Audit is qualified for three reasons: (1) budgeted figures not prepared in accordance with Canadian public sector accounting standards, (2) investment in BeeHigh Vital Elements Inc. shows impairment indicators but no write-down recorded, (3) leases on leased housing accounted for as operating rather than finance leases. Large extraordinary item of $96,455,793 caused annual surplus to spike to $103.6M vs prior year $4.86M - likely a treaty settlement or land claim. Financial statements signed April 2, 2024 for fiscal year ended March 31, 2022 indicating very late filing. Total government transfers calculated as: ISC operating $4,089,961 + ISC capital $1,107,224 + Provincial operating $298,743 + Provincial capital $12,596 + Health Canada subsidized $564,381 + CMHC subsidized $167,749 + DFO $57,479 + trust revenues $138,186 = approximately $6,436,319 plus provincial tax rebates $13,393,994 if included = $19,593,524. OSR excludes all government transfers. Restricted cash includes Land Code reserve $1,276,525 and Section 95 Housing reserve $154,064.
